message:
feat(mvcc): flush committed MVCC block versions to device on fsync and destroy

Add `MvccStore::flush_to_device()` which iterates all committed block
versions and writes the latest snapshot-visible data to the underlying
block device, ensuring in-memory MVCC writes become durable.

Wire the new flush into three call sites:

- `OpenFs::flush_mvcc_to_device()` – public convenience wrapper that
  flushes then syncs the device, returning the block count.
- ext4 and btrfs `fsync` paths – flush MVCC blocks before the existing
  `dev.sync()` call so that fsync actually persists MVCC-buffered data.
  The flushed-block count is included in the structured trace/info logs.
- `FsOps::flush_on_destroy()` trait method (default no-op) implemented
  on `OpenFs` and called from `FrankenFuse::destroy()`, guaranteeing
  all pending writes are materialised when the FUSE filesystem is
  unmounted.

Without this change, data written through the MVCC store could be lost
on unmount because the in-memory versions were never written back to the
image file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
